prerequisites: A PartnerStack vendor account with access to the program's integration settings.

A vendor API key pair (public key and private key) available under Settings > Integrations in the PartnerStack dashboard. Use production keys to read production data; test keys only operate on test customers and transactions.
Optionally, a start_date to limit how far back records are synced. If omitted, the connector reads from the earliest available data.

authMethods.name: Basic Auth (public key + private key)
authMethods.summary: The PartnerStack Vendor API authenticates with HTTP Basic Auth: the public_key is sent as the username and the private_key is sent as the password. Provide both values in the connector's public_key and private_key fields. PartnerStack maintains separate test and production key pairs; supply the pair that matches the environment you want to read.

setupSteps.title: Locate your Vendor API keys
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your PartnerStack dashboard and open Settings > Integrations. PartnerStack provides a public key and a private (secret) key for both the test and production environments. Copy the production pair to sync live partner, customer, and transaction data.

setupSteps.title: Configure connector credentials
setupSteps.body: Enter the public key into the public_key field and the private key into the private_key field. The connector uses these for HTTP Basic Auth against the Vendor API (public key as username, private key as password).

setupSteps.title: Set an optional start date
setupSteps.body: If you only need recent records, set start_date to the earliest point you want to sync from. Leave it blank to pull all available history.
